always.exam: Weight-bearing screen + Ottawa foot rules (bony tenderness navicular / 5th metatarsal base, ability to weight-bear) if any trauma
always.why: A traumatic or acute foot presentation must exclude fracture (navicular, 5th metatarsal base, Lisfranc) before loading or provocative testing.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for foot fracture requiring imaging referral

always.exam: Standing posture / arch + gait assessment (pronation, first-ray position, callus pattern)
always.why: Foot pain is strongly load- and alignment-driven; the arch/gait pattern differentiates plantar, forefoot and midfoot overload sources.

always.exam: Site-specific palpation (medial calcaneal tubercle, central heel pad, metatarsal heads, interspaces, 1st MTP, midtarsal joints)
always.why: Precise point tenderness is the primary discriminator between plantar fasciitis, fat pad syndrome, metatarsalgia, Morton's neuroma and 1st MTP pathology.

add.why: The windlass test loads the plantar fascia and is the key confirmatory test for plantar fasciopathy; calf tightness is a common driver.
Interdigital (Morton's) neuroma is confirmed by a palpable click on mediolateral squeeze with interspace pressure and altered web-space sensation.

supporting.exam: Footwear review (toe-box width, cushioning, heel height, orthotic use)
supporting.why: Footwear is a modifiable driver across plantar, forefoot and neuroma presentations and directly informs management.
Loss of 1st MTP dorsiflexion differentiates hallux rigidus/OA from a soft-tissue forefoot source.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Differentiates hallux rigidus from soft-tissue forefoot pain

assessmentLayers.Observation: Arch height, callus pattern, toe deformity (hallux valgus, claw/hammer), swelling, footwear wear
assessmentLayers.Posture: Pronation / supination, navicular drop, first-ray position, rearfoot alignment
assessmentLayers.Functional screen: Windlass on toe-stand, single-leg heel raise, gait push-off, Jack's test
assessmentLayers.STTT (Cyriax): Resisted hallux / toe flexion-extension, resisted inversion (tib-post) for arch support
assessmentLayers.CPA (compensation pattern): Intrinsic-foot / tibialis-posterior inhibition vs overactive long toe flexors
assessmentLayers.Kinetic chain: Ankle DF mobility, subtalar & midfoot mobility, tibial-rotation link upward
assessmentLayers.Fascia: Superficial back line (plantar-fascia ↔ gastroc continuity); windlass mechanism
